images.caption: Figure 3:Prefill/compression with our method. A per-layer, per-head retention gate assigns each text/visual token a scalar score; the gate’s final projection is tied across layers to normalize scores. Tokens from all layers, heads, and modalities are then pooled and globally ranked by retention, replacing fixed per-layer/per-head KV budgets.
Figure 4:PagedAttention with head-specific variable-length KV caches. Each layer–head maintains a logical KV sequence of variable length, stored as fixed-size blocks.
Figure 5:Long-reasoning performance of pure language model (Qwen3-4B).

Figure 8:Empirical evidence for AssumptionA.1. We extract the per-token query state𝐪t\mathbf{q}_{t}at layer2020, head0of Qwen3-8B for128128rollouts each on1010AIME-24 prompts, project onto the top-88PCA directions of the pooled training queries to obtain𝐫t∈ℝ8\mathbf{r}_{t}\in\mathbb{R}^{8}, and fit a per-context VAR(1) model𝐫t+1=A​𝐫t+𝐛+𝜼​t\mathbf{r}_{t+1}=A\mathbf{r}_{t}+\mathbf{b}+\boldsymbol{\eta}ton75%75\%of rollouts.(a) Stability.Eigenvalues ofAAacross all1010contexts (each context contributes88eigenvalues) lie strictly inside the unit circle; the median spectral radius isρ~=0.76\tilde{\rho}=0.76and every fitted context is stable, so the chain is mean-reverting.(b) Mean reversion / ergodicity.For one context, the per-rollout running mean1t​∑s≤t​rs​[0]\frac{1}{t}\sum{s\leq t}r_{s}[0](grey) and its ensemble average (black) converge to the fittedμ\mu(red dashed) inside a±σ\pm\sigmastationary band; the time average matches the ensemble average, consistent with ergodicity.(c) Sub-exponential innovations.Pooled log-survival of the whitened innovation magnitude|η||\eta|(blue) sits strictly between the Gaussian (red dashed) and exponential (green dotted) reference curves, satisfying the sub-exponential tail condition that drives the persistence bound.

Figure 9:Token attention-survival under full-cache inference.Top row: Qwen3-4B on100100OpenR1-Math prefills up toT=16,384T=16{,}384tokens.Bottom row: Qwen3-VL-4B on9898MMDU multimodal dialogues (text++interleaved images), prefilled to the same length.Left column: survival rate averaged across all layers and query heads.Right column: survival rate for a single representative head (Math: layer1212, head1717; MMDU: layer1515, head2424). For each query positionppwe identify the head’s selected set of past tokens using either the top-KKlogits (K∈64,128,256,512K\in{64,128,256,512}) or the smallest set whose softmax mass reaches a thresholdτ∈0.90,0.95,0.99\tau\in{0.90,0.95,0.99}; a token of birth timeiiis then alive at horizonLLif it appears in the selected set of some query at positionp≥i+Lp\geq i+L. The dashed vertical line marksL=4096L=4096. In every panel, every fixed top-KKcurve drops sharply withLL, while the more lenient mass criteria stay substantially higher: the gap between top-KKandτ\tau-mass curves is the attention-dilution mass — tokens that still carry meaningful aggregate weight but are pushed out of the head’s hard top-KKbudget. Heterogeneity at the head level is large: a representative head on either dataset loses the majority of tokens byL=4096L=4096even under the most lenient criterion (99%99\%mass), in contrast to the across-head average. The same qualitative shape appears in the multimodal setting (rows c, d), confirming that attention dilution is not specific to text-only inputs.

Figure 10:Efficiency scaling with context length. The figure reports throughput in tokens per second (left) and total decoding time in seconds (right) as the input context length increases. Evaluated methods include Vanilla (Full Cache), DBTrimKV, and TrimKV. We fix batch size at 16, number of generated tokens at 8192 and KV budget at 256. Results are averaged over 5 random seeds.

Figure 11:Efficiency scaling with generation length. The figure reports throughput in tokens per second (left) and total decoding time in seconds (right) as the number of generated tokens increases. Evaluated methods include Vanilla (Full Cache), DBTrimKV, and TrimKV. We fix batch size at 16, context length at 16384 and KV budget at 256. We do not report vanilla performance for generation length at 32k due to OOM error. Results are averaged over 5 random seeds.

Figure 12:Efficiency scaling with KV budget. The figure reports throughput in tokens per second (left) and total decoding time in seconds (right) across different allocated KV memory budgets. Evaluated methods include Vanilla (Full Cache), DBTrimKV, and TrimKV. We fix batch size at 16, number of generated tokens at 8192 and number of generated tokens at 8192. Results are averaged over 5 random seeds.
